name: DNA Repair Enhancement surveillance coverage assessment for June 10, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Surveillance window from the published DNA Repair Enhancement baseline through the June 10 field-change check.

summary: The baseline is publishable but still thin. The June 10 surveillance pass found a material DREAM repair-regulator source that merits review, while also showing that systemic DNA repair regulators, nucleotide/NAD boundary records, disease-specific DNA repair syndrome trials, safety, and recent review context need a dedicated coverage-repair pass.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline covers oral nicotinamide skin-cancer chemoprevention, topical DNA repair enzyme or photolyase skin studies, and indirect NR/NMN human trials.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Human evidence remains tissue-specific or indirect; systemic aging-relevant DNA repair endpoints are still sparse.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged DREAM study/finding update adds animal mutation-burden biology and cross-mammal lifespan association to this track, but there is not yet a druglike animal healthspan intervention record in the public map.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Coverage repair should decide whether additional animal DNA repair enhancement studies belong in this track.

evidence_categories.rationale: Current records cover skin UV DNA damage, nicotinamide repair-proximal mechanisms, indirect NAD biology, and staged DREAM repression biology.
The ONTRANS null trial and the NR/NMN no-DNA-repair-endpoint findings are already represented as limiting context.
Available human safety context is short, skin-local, disease-specific, or indirect; systemic repair manipulation and off-target genome-response risks remain underbuilt.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Coverage repair should make safety and off-target genome-response limitations explicit if additional systemic repair-regulator evidence is added.

evidence_categories.rationale: Known result-bearing registries were re-checked, but close NAD/nucleotide and DNA repair disease-boundary registries are not fully mapped for this track.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Review NCT06465602, NCT06592859, NCT07144527, NCT06324877, NCT04870866, NCT06208527, and other NAD or DNA repair syndrome boundary trials during coverage repair.

evidence_categories.rationale: The surveillance pass identified a recent DNA repair targeting review, but it was not staged because the public update is anchored to a same-window primary source.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Review PMID 42227145 and related 2026 reviews during coverage repair to improve source-completeness and boundary mapping.

evidence_categories.rationale: The pass exposed recurring boundary issues around NAD precursors, exogenous nucleotides, DNA repair syndromes, oncology DDR inhibitors, telomere biology, and skin photoprotection.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Coverage repair should state which boundary sources belong in DNA Repair Enhancement versus NAD and Redox Restoration, Telomere Restoration, disease-specific oncology, or ordinary photoprotection.

search_log_summary: PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, UMIN, and ANZCTR discovery found one material same-window primary source for public update and multiple close-but-unstaged boundary records.
source_selection_notes: PMID 42230995 was selected because it is a same-window primary source directly tied to DNA repair repression and somatic mutation burden.
PMID 42265534 was excluded from the staged update because exogenous nucleotide supplementation moved DNAmAge and telomere endpoints but did not directly measure DNA repair enhancement.
NCT07144527 and related NMN/NR registries are already better represented in NAD and Redox Restoration unless future results directly measure DNA repair endpoints.

known_gaps.description: Human evidence remains concentrated in UV-exposed skin and indirect NAD biology rather than systemic aging-relevant DNA repair endpoints.
known_gaps.suggested_action: During coverage repair, search for direct human DNA repair endpoint studies outside skin and explicitly exclude wrong-scope disease or oncology sources.

known_gaps.description: DREAM introduces broader repair-regulator biology, but the public map does not yet cover the surrounding preclinical DNA repair enhancement literature.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Review DREAM, SIRT6, DNA glycosylase, ATM/ATR, and related repair-regulator sources for track fit.

known_gaps.description: NAD precursors, exogenous nucleotides, NMN/NR registries, and telomere endpoints overlap with DNA repair language but often lack direct repair endpoints.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Map NAD/nucleotide sources to DNA Repair Enhancement only when direct DNA repair endpoints or repair-regulator claims justify it.

known_gaps.description: Systemic repair enhancement could carry off-target genome-response or cancer-biology risks that are not yet well represented in the public outlook.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Add safety and durability boundaries if coverage repair stages systemic repair-regulator records.

next_coverage_action: After evidence review and publication of the DREAM surveillance bundle, run coverage repair for DNA Repair Enhancement with emphasis on systemic repair-regulator biology, NAD/nucleotide boundary records, active registries, recent reviews, and safety/off-target genome-response limits.
